message.content:
PLAYER: "Scrodinger" (multiple accounts).
RATINGS: chess.com rapid 2010 (main: scrodingxxf) & 2000 (alt); lichess blitz 1707, rapid 1754, classical 1771; chess.com blitz best 1612, currently lower; bullet ~1150.
TACTICS/PUZZLES: chess.com tactics 2073-2212 (a genuine strength), lichess puzzles 2073, Puzzle Rush 48/51.
ESTIMATED FIDE OTB: ~1600-1700. Has NOT played FIDE-rated OTB yet.
REPERTOIRE — White: 1.e4 player. vs 1...e5 Italian Game (wants to add Ruy Lopez); vs Sicilian plays Open Sicilian (inconsistent); vs French Tarrasch 3.Nd2; vs Caro-Kann Advance 3.e5.
REPERTOIRE — Black: vs 1.e4 Sicilian (various lines, primary weapon but inconsistent — wants to settle on Najdorf); vs 1.d4 Nimzo-Indian (E43); occasional Scandinavian/Caro (secondary, should drop).
STRENGTHS: strong tactical eye; good rapid calculation; very fast improver (+400 in 18 months); aggressive, plays for the win.
WEAKNESSES (priority order): (1) Black win rate 32% vs White 48% — Black repertoire inconsistent; (2) king safety — loses to coordinated middlegame attacks; (3) blitz 500+ pts below rapid — calculation not yet automatic under time pressure; (4) middlegame transition after the opening — loses the thread converting to a positional game; (5) endgame technique likely undertrained; (6) no stable OTB rating.
GOAL: become a very strong player on the path toward titles (CM -> FM -> IM -> GM over years). Wants a personalized course/resource curriculum.


YOU ARE AN ADVERSARIAL FACT-CHECKER for chess resources. Category: "Endgame Mastery".

Here are researched resources as JSON:
[
 {
  "name": "Silman's Complete Endgame Course: From Beginner to Master",
  "rtype": "Book",
  "provider": "Jeremy Silman (Silman-James Press)",
  "url": "https://www.silmanjamespress.com/shop/chess/silmans-complete-endgame-course/",
  "cost": "~$29.95 USD (print); Kindle ~$15.99",
  "level": "1600-2300 (rating-tiered chapters)",
  "why": "This is THE foundational fix for Scrodinger's #5 weakness (undertrained endgame technique). Its unique rating-band structure lets him study exactly the 'Class A / Expert' tier matching his 2010 rapid / ~1600-1700 estimated FIDE and skip what he already knows - efficient for a fast improver who needs to plug a specific gap, not relearn basics.",
  "priority": "Essential",
  "time": "1 book, ~6-10 weeks reading the tiers up to his level"
 },
 {
  "name": "100 Endgames You Must Know",
  "rtype": "Online Course",
  "provider": "GM Jesus de la Villa (Chessable / New in Chess)",
  "url": "https://www.chessable.com/100-endgames-you-must-know/course/5193/",
  "cost": "~$29.99-$39.99 USD (free book-text tier; paid adds video)",
  "level": "1600-2200",
  "why": "Directly targets weakness #4 (losing the thread when converting to a positional/technical game) and #5. Chessable's spaced-repetition move trainer turns the 100 theoretical wins/draws into automatic recall - the same drilling that made his tactics a 2073-2212 strength, now applied to endgames. Knowing these 100 lets him confidently steer his aggressive games into won endings instead of fearing the conversion.",
  "priority": "Essential",
  "time": "20-40 hrs initial, then ongoing spaced reviews"
 },
 {
  "name": "Principles of Chess Endgames (Beginner to Master series)",
  "rtype": "YouTube Channel",
  "provider": "GM Daniel Naroditsky",
  "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QUqq7wSLE78",
  "cost": "Free",
  "level": "1500-2300",
  "why": "Naroditsky explains the WHY behind pawn breakthroughs, king activity and N-vs-B endings - exactly the middlegame-to-endgame transition judgment (weakness #4) that books alone don't build. Free and verbal, it complements Silman's text and is ideal passive study for a player who already calculates well but needs the strategic narrative of when an endgame is winning.",
  "priority": "Recommended",
  "time": "~10-15 hrs across the series, watch alongside book study"
 },
 {
  "name": "Lichess Practice: Endgame studies (Pawn & Rook Endgames)",
  "rtype": "Platform",
  "provider": "Lichess.org",
  "url": "https://lichess.org/practice",
  "cost": "Free",
  "level": "1500-2100",
  "why": "Free interactive drilling of opposition, key squares, 7th-rank rook pawns and basic/practical rook endings against instant feedback. Scrodinger already lives on lichess (puzzles 2073) - this lets him build the technical muscle memory that addresses weakness #3 (calculation not automatic under time pressure) so simple endgames don't bleed his blitz rating.",
  "priority": "Essential",
  "time": "15-30 min sessions, ongoing"
 },
 {
  "name": "Chess.com Endgame Drills: Fundamentals & Practice",
  "rtype": "Software/Tool",
  "provider": "Chess.com",
  "url": "https://www.chess.com/drills/endgame-fundamentals",
  "cost": "First few drills free; full access via Chess.com membership (~$5-14/mo)",
  "level": "1600-2200",
  "why": "Practice-against-the-engine drills with move-quality feedback, on the same platform where his main rating lives (rapid 2010, scrodingxxf). Repeating these converts theoretical knowledge into the automatic technique he lacks when the clock is low - reinforcing the fix for weaknesses #3 and #5.",
  "priority": "Recommended",
  "time": "ongoing, a few drills daily"
 },
 {
  "name": "Fundamental Chess Endings",
  "rtype": "Book",
  "provider": "GM Karsten Muller & IM Frank Lamprecht (Gambit)",
  "url": "http://www.gambitbooks.com/books/Fundamental_Chess_Endings.html",
  "cost": "~$34.95 USD (print); Kindle/ebook ~$19.99",
  "level": "1900-2400",
  "why": "The modern one-volume reference (the one young Carlsen drilled). For a player explicitly aiming CM->FM->IM->GM, this is the comprehensive bridge between Silman and Dvoretsky - organized by material so he can look up exactly the rook or minor-piece ending he just botched. Use as a reference manual to systematically close weakness #5 as his level climbs.",
  "priority": "Recommended",
  "time": "reference use over 1-2 years"
 },
 {
  "name": "Dvoretsky's Endgame Manual (5th Edition, rev. Karsten Muller)",
  "rtype": "Book",
  "provider": "Mark Dvoretsky (Russell Enterprises)",
  "url": "https://www.russell-enterprises.com/russell-enterprises/dvoretskys-endgame-manual",
  "cost": "~$34.95 USD print (also Chessable ~$29.99-$39.99; ForwardChess 6th ed.)",
  "level": "2100-2600",
  "why": "The acknowledged advanced standard ('a must for every chess professional' - Kramnik). This is the long-term capstone for Scrodinger's titled-player goal - the blue-text precise-knowledge sections are exactly the deep technique an IM/GM aspirant must master. Not for now, but the destination after Silman + 100 Endgames; tackle once he's a stable 2100+.",
  "priority": "Optional",
  "time": "a multi-year project; revisit when 2100+"
 },
 {
  "name": "Endgame Lectures with GM Yasser Seirawan",
  "rtype": "Video Series",
  "provider": "Saint Louis Chess Club",
  "url": "https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLVWaFpMwtaGiVZ77NhhvGGGzvF7oFSWcA",
  "cost": "Free",
  "level": "1700-2300",
  "why": "GM-level annotated endgame instruction from real master games, free. Watching how a GM treats the transition into and through the endgame trains weakness #4 (losing the positional thread after the opening) - it shows the strategic plan an aggressive 1.e4 player needs once the attack fizzles and the game must be won technically.",
  "priority": "Optional",
  "time": "~10-20 hrs, casual viewing"
 }
]

For EACH resource: use WebSearch/WebFetch to confirm it genuinely exists, the author/provider is correct, it is currently available, and the price is roughly right. Correct any wrong URL/price/author/level. Set verified=true ONLY if you confirmed it real and accurately described; otherwise verified=false with a note. Default to skepticism: if you cannot confirm a specific course/book really exists, mark verified=false and confidence=low. Drop obvious hallucinations entirely. Also re-check that each "why" is genuinely personalized to Scrodinger and tighten it if weak. Reputable, well-known resources (Silman, Dvoretsky, Vukovic, Naroditsky, Saint Louis CC, established Chessable bestsellers) can be high-confidence. Return the cleaned list.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- contribute:contribute: This skill should be used when the user asks to "contribute to open source", "find an issue to work on", "analyze a repo", "submit a pull request", "review PR feedback", "triage an issue", "sync my fork", "create a release", "clean up contribution branches", "debug CI failures", or mentions open-source contribution workflows. Provides a full-lifecycle open-source contribution system with 12 phases.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Links: [{"title":"Using Your King | Beginner to Master | Principles of Chess Endgames | GM Naroditsky - YouTube","url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=n3FufrVltsc"},{"title":"Advanced Pawn Endgame Concepts | Beginner to Master | Principles of Chess Endgames | GM Naroditsky - YouTube","url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Fxjthnv7mBQ"},{"title":"Introduction to Pawn Endgames | Principles of Chess Endgames | GM Naroditsky - YouTube","url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QUqq7wSLE78"},{"title":"End Game - YouTube","url":"https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLT1F2nOxLHOfQI_hFiDnnWj4lb5KsviJ_"},{"title":"About - Daniel Naroditsky Chess Opening Explorer | Chess Memory","url":"https://chess-memory.com/about"},{"title":"Master Endgame Chess Strategies ft. Andrea Botez | GM Daniel Naroditsky - YouTube","url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=m0zMjxe2Vgc"},{"title":"Blazing A Path","url":"https://uscf1-nyc1.aodhosting.com/CL-AND-CR-ALL/CL-ALL/naroditsky/The_Practical_Endgame_2014-2020.pdf"},{"title":"Mastering Complex Endgames by Daniel Naroditsky (Ebook) - Read free for 30 days","url":"https://www.everand.com/book/951183242/Mastering-Complex-Endgames-Practical-Lessons-on-Critical-Ideas-Plans"}]

Based on the search results, I found several relevant resources related to Daniel Naroditsky's endgame content:

## YouTube Videos

Naroditsky has created videos on pawn endgames with detailed overviews of proper king use. Some specific videos in his "Principles of Chess Endgames" series include:

1. "Introduction to Pawn Endgames," which initiates his journey into the endgame, featuring one of his inspiring endgames
2. "Using Your King" (published October 19, 2022)
3. "Advanced Pawn Endgame Concepts" (published October 23, 2022)

## Series Overview

Naroditsky is known for his YouTube speedrun series including "Speedrun to 3000," "Beginner to Master," "Sensei Speedrun," and "Master Class" series. His YouTube speedrun series has helped millions of chess players improve by demonstrating proper chess opening theory, middlegame strategy, and endgame technique at every rating level.

## Additional Resources

A comprehensive database exists featuring every position from Naroditsky's "Beginner to Master" series and other speedrun content, which provides direct links to his YouTube explanations with timestamps.

You can find these videos directly on his YouTube channel or through chess learning platforms that aggregate his content.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Web search results for query: "Lichess practice endgame studies pawn rook endgames lichess.org/practice"

Links: [{"title":"Endgame Studies • lichess.org","url":"https://lichess.org/study/topic/Endgame%20Studies/alphabetical"},{"title":"Practice: Rook And Pawn Endgames • lichess.org","url":"https://lichess.org/study/dqCpuvFS/eQLYGkny"},{"title":"Rook vs Pawn Endgame • lichess.org","url":"https://lichess.org/study/FsNaGX3J/leWsi6QE"},{"title":"Rook and Pawn Endgames • lichess.org","url":"https://lichess.org/study/topic/Rook%20and%20Pawn%20Endgames/popular"},{"title":"Chess Endgame Training","url":"https://chess-endgame-trainer.web.app/"},{"title":"Basic Rook Endgames • lichess.org","url":"https://lichess.org/practice/rook-endgames/basic-rook-endgames/pqUSUw8Y/tAcIEIbG"},{"title":"Basic endgame trainer - Part 1 (rook and pawn) • lichess.org","url":"https://lichess.org/study/oHIWyzx8"},{"title":"Endgames • lichess.org","url":"https://lichess.org/study/vMmllPNw"},{"title":"Pawn Endgames 🧩 • lichess.org","url":"https://lichess.org/study/xPasBcWZ/8Vp0bFLu"}]

I found several relevant results for Lichess endgame practice studies focusing on rook and pawn endgames:

**Key Resources on Lichess:**

Lichess offers endgame studies including rook and pawn endgames (Rpp vs R), with various difficulty levels available.

There are studies covering important rook and pawn endgame concepts like rook placement, king activity, shouldering maneuvers, and classic positions like Philidor's position, Euwe's position, and Lucena's position.

**Specific Practice Topics Available:**

- Practice: Rook And Pawn Endgames by jomega
- Basic endgame trainer - Part 1 (rook and pawn) by AACtrl
- Rook vs Pawn Endgame studies

You can access these studies directly on Lichess at their endgame studies section. The platform provides interactive practice positions where you can learn and test your understanding of critical rook and pawn endgame techniques.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text: Naroditsky series confirmed; the provided URL (QUqq7wSLE78) is the real "Introduction to Pawn Endgames" video. Lichess Practice confirmed — note the official endgame practice lives at lichess.org/practice with categories like rook endgames. Let me check the remaining four.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about chess.com's endgame drills and practice:

## Chess.com Endgame Fundamentals Drills

Chess.com offers Endgame Fundamentals chess drills to improve your chess, with 81 drills available. You can improve your chess skills by practicing endgames drills through their platform.

## Membership & Access

Access to the first few drills in each section is free, and access to the rest can be purchased through monthly or annual subscription.

## Content Coverage

The endgame drills cover fundamental concepts including:
- King and pawn endgames
- Opposition and zugzwang techniques
- The active king in endgames
- Triangulation methods
- Drawing and winning strategies

The king and pawn drills will make more sense if you've done tutorials on Q + K vs K mate, opposition, and K + P vs K endgames, and the drills assume you are already familiar with the concepts that the drill is helping you practice.

The endgame fundamentals section on chess.com is designed to help players master essential endgame patterns and techniques through interactive practice drills.

content.text:
All eight resources confirmed real. Key corrections:

- **Dvoretsky**: 5th ed confirmed at Russell Enterprises, $34.95. Note: a 6th edition is forthcoming and a Carlsen-foreword edition exists. The ISBN 9781949859188 is the 5th ed. Good.
- **Seirawan playlist**: The provided URL is the general "Lectures with GM Yasser Seirawan" playlist, NOT an endgame-specific playlist. The resource title "Endgame Lectures" overstates this — it's a mixed-topic lectures playlist that includes some endgame content. I'll correct the name and lower confidence slightly / note this.
- **Fundamental Chess Endings**: "young Carlsen drilled it" is unverifiable lore — softening.
- **Chess.com drills**: canonical URL updated.
- **Silman price**: list ~$24.95, not $29.95.

Now I'll submit the cleaned, verified list.

resources.name: Silman's Complete Endgame Course: From Beginner to Master
resources.why: THE foundational fix for Scrodinger's #5 weakness (undertrained endgame technique). Its rating-band structure lets him jump straight to the 'Class A / Expert' tier matching his 2010 rapid (~1600-1700 estimated FIDE) and skip the basics he already knows - efficient for a fast improver plugging one specific gap rather than relearning.
resources.time: 1 book, ~6-10 weeks reading the tiers up to his level
resources.note: Confirmed real on publisher site and Amazon (ISBN 1890085103). Corrected list price from ~$29.95 to ~$24.95 (publisher list); print commonly sells $18-25. Also exists as a Chessable course. Author/title/level accurate.

resources.name: 100 Endgames You Must Know
resources.provider: GM Jesus de la Villa (Chessable / New in Chess)
resources.cost: ~$29.99-$39.99 USD (free book-text tier; paid PRO adds video/extras)
resources.why: Directly targets weakness #4 (losing the thread when converting to a positional/technical game) and #5. Chessable's spaced-repetition trainer turns 100 theoretical wins/draws into automatic recall - the exact drilling that made his tactics a 2073-2212 strength, now applied to endgames so his aggressive games can be steered into won endings instead of fearing the conversion.
resources.time: 20-40 hrs initial, then ongoing spaced reviews
resources.note: Confirmed on Chessable (course 5193) and the underlying New in Chess book (de la Villa). A separate Workbook course also exists. Author/provider/level accurate.

resources.name: Principles of Chess Endgames (Beginner to Master series)
resources.why: Naroditsky explains the WHY behind pawn breakthroughs, king activity and N-vs-B endings - exactly the middlegame-to-endgame transition judgment (weakness #4) that books alone don't build. Free and verbal, it complements Silman's text and suits a player who already calculates well but needs the strategic narrative of when an endgame is winning.
resources.time: ~10-15 hrs across the series, watch alongside book study
resources.note: Confirmed: the provided URL is the real 'Introduction to Pawn Endgames | Principles of Chess Endgames | GM Naroditsky' video, part of a genuine multi-video series on his channel. Reputable instructor.

resources.name: Lichess Practice: Endgames (Pawn & Rook Endgames)
resources.why: Free interactive drilling of opposition, key squares, the Lucena/Philidor positions and basic/practical rook endings with instant feedback. Scrodinger already lives on lichess (puzzles 2073), so this builds the technical muscle memory that addresses weakness #3 (calculation not automatic under time pressure) - so simple endgames stop bleeding his blitz rating.
resources.note: Confirmed: lichess.org/practice hosts official interactive endgame categories including 'Basic rook endgames' and pawn-endgame sets. Trimmed name slightly ('studies' replaced with 'Endgames' to match the Practice section, distinct from user-made Studies). Free, accurate.

resources.name: Chess.com Endgame Drills: Fundamentals & Practice
resources.cost: First few drills per section free; full access via Chess.com membership (~$5-14/mo)
resources.why: Practice-against-the-engine drills with move-quality feedback, on the very platform where his main rating lives (rapid 2010, scrodingxxf). Repeating these converts theoretical knowledge into the automatic technique he lacks when the clock is low - reinforcing the fix for weaknesses #3 and #5.
resources.note: Confirmed real (81 endgame-fundamentals drills). Updated URL to the current canonical path chess.com/practice/drills/endgame-fundamentals (the old /drills/ path still resolves). Freemium model accurate.

resources.name: Fundamental Chess Endings
resources.provider: GM Karsten Muller & IM Frank Lamprecht (Gambit Publications)
resources.why: The modern one-volume reference, organized by material so Scrodinger can look up exactly the rook or minor-piece ending he just botched. For a player explicitly aiming CM->FM->IM->GM, it is the comprehensive bridge between Silman and Dvoretsky - a manual to systematically close weakness #5 as his level climbs.
resources.note: Confirmed on Gambit's site and Amazon (ISBN 9781901983531, Muller & Lamprecht, 2001, ~416pp). Removed the unverifiable 'young Carlsen drilled it' anecdote from the why. Price range widened to reflect current market.

resources.name: Dvoretsky's Endgame Manual (5th Edition, rev. Karsten Muller)
resources.why: The acknowledged advanced standard ('a must for every chess professional' - Kramnik). This is the long-term capstone for Scrodinger's titled-player goal - the blue-text precise-knowledge sections are exactly the deep technique an IM/GM aspirant must master. Not for now: the destination after Silman + 100 Endgames, once he is a stable 2100+.
resources.note: Confirmed at Russell Enterprises, $34.95, 5th ed. revised by Muller (ISBN 9781949859188). Note: a 6th edition is forthcoming and a separate Carlsen-foreword printing exists - the 5th remains the standard current edition. Accurate.

resources.name: Lectures with GM Yasser Seirawan (Saint Louis Chess Club)
resources.why: GM-level annotated instruction from real master games, free. Seirawan's lectures on classic games show how a GM treats the transition into and through the endgame - training weakness #4 (losing the positional thread after the opening) by modeling the technical plan an aggressive 1.e4 player needs once the attack fizzles and the game must be won by conversion.
resources.note: Playlist confirmed real, but it is the GENERAL 'Lectures with GM Yasser Seirawan' playlist (mixed topics: openings, pawn structure, classic games), NOT an endgame-only series. Corrected the name and reworded the why to reflect that endgame content is a subset, not the whole playlist - hence medium confidence on it being the best endgame-specific pick.

content.text:
All eight resources verified as genuine. Summary of fact-check findings:

**All confirmed real and available** — no hallucinations to drop. Corrections made:

1. **Silman's Complete Endgame Course** — price corrected from ~$29.95 to ~$24.95 (publisher list). High confidence.
2. **100 Endgames You Must Know** — confirmed (Chessable course 5193, de la Villa). High confidence.
3. **Naroditsky Principles of Chess Endgames** — the provided URL is the real "Introduction to Pawn Endgames" video in a genuine series. High confidence.
4. **Lichess Practice** — confirmed at lichess.org/practice; trimmed name to match the official Practice section (distinct from user Studies). High confidence.
5. **Chess.com Endgame Drills** — confirmed (81 drills); URL updated to current canonical path `/practice/drills/endgame-fundamentals`. High confidence.
6. **Fundamental Chess Endings** — confirmed (Muller & Lamprecht, Gambit, ISBN 9781901983531); removed the unverifiable "young Carlsen drilled it" anecdote. High confidence.
7. **Dvoretsky's Endgame Manual** — confirmed 5th ed. at Russell Enterprises, $34.95; noted a 6th edition is forthcoming. High confidence.
8. **Seirawan lectures** — most significant correction: the playlist is the **general** mixed-topic Seirawan lecture series, NOT an endgame-only series. Renamed and reworded the rationale; dropped to **medium confidence** since it's not the cleanest endgame-specific pick.

All `why` fields were tightened and kept tied to Scrodinger's specific weaknesses (#3 calculation under time pressure, #4 middlegame-to-endgame transition, #5 undertrained endgames) and his profile (2010 rapid, lichess habits, titled-player goal).
